notes: Steam Controller now works if you set the Controller Configuration to use emulate the Keyboard and Mouse, which I couldn't get to work in Windows 10. However, the game will show keyboard prompts, as with any game you do this with. Otherwise, runs perfectly smooth.

notes: Any issues I have with this game (50fps framelock, no 1920x1080 option, controller input prompts just show generic Xinput "button1, button2, button3 etc") are all present on Windows. It runs exactly how it does on Windows.
title: Disney•Pixar Toy Story 3: The Video Game

notes: Perfect compatibility, the game has a few issues but they're not related to Proton and ARE PRESENT ON WINDOWS. Here are some: Game runs at a max of about 1600x900, and with a framecap of 50fps. No idea why. Game shows Xinput button prompts (Button1, Button2, etc) instead of actual labelled controller prompts such as A, B, X, Y, Start, Select (tested with my Steam Controller). Mouse has a bit of lag and stutter when moving it around on menus. The game runs pretty much identical to how it runs on Windows, including the issues present there too.
title: Disney•Pixar Toy Story 3: The Video Game

notes: Played the entire game with Proton 4.11-6, everything works perfectly, just like on Windows.
title: Disney•Pixar Toy Story 3: The Video Game
